programmes.show_description: How is NYC supplied with the finest steaks? How do they produce the ultimate surround sound home cinema systems? S9, Ep17
How is viscose transformed into clothing and cloth? How do they produce the world's favourite fizzy drinks? How do they make traditional English snuff? S9, Ep18
Scott's VW Campervan. Scott would love to own a VW Campervan to take his son on trips around their native Scotland. With Camper prices soaring, Mike and Elvis must be creative. S2, Ep3
Simon's BMW Z4. Mike and Elvis try to buy carpenter Simon his dream BMW Z4. Simon only has £1,500 saved up and his daily motor is one of the worst they've ever seen. S2, Ep4
Everything But The Kitchen Sink. In Cornwall, Henry and Sam visit a trials car enthusiast with an eclectic set of sheds. Will they come away with a jeep, a bike or something else? S2, Ep3
A Right Royal Profit? Henry and Sam are in Cornwall visiting the Queen's armourer, Terry English, and his amazing collection of vintage cars, bikes and engines. What will they find? S2, Ep4
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as micro drill bits and skiffs made? S26, Ep22
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as ocean drone transformers and 3D puzzles made? S26, Ep23
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Organ builder David tries to rescue a vintage harmonium. Kirsten works on a ceramic dish, while Jay leads the repair of a hand-crafted doll's house. S4, Ep13
Portugal Special. Drew and Tee take a trip to Portugal, a hidden gem of the antiques trade. They unearth some architectural marvels recovered from an 18th century church. S18, Ep1
Every Day Is A School Day. Drew never knows what he might find, who he'll meet, or what he'll learn. He visits an outsider artist with a newly curated art collection. S18, Ep2
The Mahoneys spend thousands of dollars on a loader for a final push at 8-Mile, and Alex is convinced there's big gold at Relief Creek. The Gold Gypsies strike it lucky. S3, Ep11
With the season close to ending, the Dirt Dogs struggle to hunt out their final $350,000 of gold. The Mahoneys bring in machinery in a final effort to hit their target. S3, Ep12
A secret map once used by the RAF navigates David to potential profits and a 350-year-old book gets Roo's attention. Who'll win the final auction in North Shields? S22, Ep5
In Scotland, Angus and the team clear out an attic filled with Ferrari memorabilia. How much will this incredible collection make at auction? S1, Ep5
Organ builder David tries to rescue a vintage harmonium. Kirsten works on a ceramic dish, while Jay leads the repair of a hand-crafted doll's house. S4, Ep13
Classic, Rare And Beautiful. In the West Country, Henry and Sam find some sheds full of classic bikes and vintage cars. They even find a limousine once owned by Eva Peron. S2, Ep5
Yorkshires' Finest. Henry and Sam visit a fanatical biker in Yorkshire, who has the biggest collection of motorcycles they've seen so far. What bike will they come away with? S2, Ep6
Tristan. In Chelmsford, Henry and Fuzz visit Fiat specialist Tristan, who has 500s, 600s, 126s and a Fiat minivan that once starred in an Indiana Jones movie. S6, Ep9
Dominic. The boys are in Oxfordshire browsing through Dominic's restoration projects, including a Triumph TR4, a Jaguar XJS and a hotrod with a Rover engine. S6, Ep10
Tongs Will Wag! Quirky items, including a secret society's ballot box, an uncle's gold tooth, and expensive kitchenware, create a buzz in the auction room. S6, Ep6
He Who Dares Sells. Angus helps an RAF hero sell a rare SAS diary to raise cash for veterans. Also, a man parts with 250 cars, hoping to fund the cruise of a lifetime. S6, Ep7
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep4
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep5
Sacred Secret & Saucy Surprise. Angus sells the treasures of an Abbey to fund the monks' retirement, and a motoring fanatic's cheeky sculpture sets pulses racing on sale day. S6, Ep20
Experts Joe and Steve work together to bring a vintage cash register back to life. Plus, Janine visits wood expert Will in the hopes he can restore her doll house. S5, Ep37
Woodwork expert Will attempts to save a valuable music box that has been in Pamela's family for over 50 years. Plus, Steve fixes up a precious railway lamp. S5, Ep38
How do they build the world's most expensive cable cars? How is vodka produced from grass? How do social networks connect people around the world? S9, Ep19
How do skydiving suits work? How do they produce Surströmming, one of the world's smelliest foods? How do they transform weeds into walls? S9, Ep20
How do they mine precious opals beneath one of the hottest deserts on earth? How do they build breath-taking stunt planes and how do they make nougat? S10, Ep1
How do they farm the expensive spice saffron, how do they create giant roads made entirely from ice and how do they manufacture a muscle car? S10, Ep2
Kate's Mini Countryman. Mike and Elvis are determined to buy a Mini Countryman for a caring mother. But the boys face a tough task with £500 to spend and no trade-in car. S2, Ep5
Jordan's Subaru Impreza. The boys desperately want to buy policeman Jordan his dream car, a Subaru Impreza. Elvis surprises Mike as he dips into his contact book to buy a kit car. S2, Ep6
Classic, Rare And Beautiful. In the West Country, Henry and Sam find some sheds full of classic bikes and vintage cars. They even find a limousine once owned by Eva Peron. S2, Ep5
Yorkshires' Finest. Henry and Sam visit a fanatical biker in Yorkshire, who has the biggest collection of motorcycles they've seen so far. What bike will they come away with? S2, Ep6
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including Formula F race cars made? S26, Ep24
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including vacuum excavators made? S26, Ep25
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A Mysterious Find. When four sisters need advice about what to do with their father's 1939 328 BMW, they call on Wayne. But importantly, will he be able to make them a profit? S6, Ep13
What Wayne Wants. Wayne Carini welcomes viewers into the world of high-end car collecting. He must auction off a number of vehicles in his quest to obtain a 1911 Winton 17b. S6, Ep14
Red Hot and Rowdy. Wayne Carini welcomes viewers into the world of high-end car collecting. A woman turns to Wayne for help in selling her late husband's 1965 Shelby Cobra 427 S/C. S6, Ep15
Chance of a Lifetime. Wayne welcomes viewers into the world of high-end car collecting. He gets a personal look at the last remaining original 1967 Ferrari 275 GTB/4 N.A.R.T. Spyder. S6, Ep16
Fine And Landy. Mike's found a British motoring icon: a 1976 Land Rover Series III. One of the few registered in California, it's a rare find but Ant has work to do to get it moving. S16, Ep12
The British Are Leaving. Mike and Ant look back on their American adventure. Before they return home to the UK, they pack up the workshop and sort through the work of half a decade. S16, Ep16
Try, Try, Triumph. To Ant's dismay, Mike buys a 1979 Triumph TR7. Underneath its disco-era styling are some quirks, like a homemade air cleaner, bad wiring and soft shocks. S16, Ep2
TVR S2 - Part 1. Mike heads to Yorkshire to see a TVR S up for auction. Can he pull off the winning bid and get the car back in one piece for Edd to work his magic on? S6, Ep15
TVR S2 - Part 2. Edd has been working hard to get a TVR S back on the starting blocks. But when more problems arise, will it be ready in time for Mike to sell for a profit? S6, Ep16
Morgan. Mike is seduced by a stunning Morgan Plus 4 at only £13,000, but it needs a new chassis and fitting it will take at least a hundred man hours. How will Edd react? S9, Ep2
TVR. Mike tracks down a TVR Cerbera for just £8,000. However, these classic sports cars are susceptible to rust. Can Edd patch it up or will the chassis need replacing? S10, Ep6
Aston Martin DB7. Mike gets a great deal on a rundown Aston Martin DB7. Can Edd restore this classic at a profit - or will hidden problems blow their budget? S10, Ep1
Dave Parkin. Henry and Fuzz visit Dave to acquire his Honda C90 and Gaz-69 Jeep. They're determined to revive both machines and return them to road-ready glory. S7, Ep20
From Cornwall To Oxfordshire. Henry Cole and motorcycle guru Sam Lovegrove travel from Cornwall to Oxfordshire to rummage in some of Britain's most amazing sheds and make some cash. S1, Ep1
Beach Buggy. In Leicestershire, Henry and Fuzz explore a shed full of classics, selecting a 1970s VW Beetle Beach Buggy. Can they restore it in time for a Somerset beach ride? S1, Ep1
Mini. In Wiltshire, Henry and Fuzz admire Morris Minors and vintage mopeds. Can they revive a late 1980s Austin Mini in time for a historical road trip to Oxford? S1, Ep2
Model A Ford. In awe of a huge collection of classic cars from all eras in Northamptonshire, Henry and Fuzz decide to transform a 1920s Model A Ford pickup truck. S1, Ep3
American Dream. The Cogs bid for car-show glory with an iconic American pick-up truck. Richard is back on the racetrack. Can he impress the classic car power-players? S5, Ep5
Metalwork expert Dominic restores an incredible 100-year-old banana barrow. Meanwhile, leather work expert Suzie repairs a well-used wallet with a remarkable history. S5, Ep48
The team of restorers revive treasured heirlooms including a 1960s lawnmower, a Welsh rugby union trophy and a personalised leather belt. S8, Ep10
Sacred Secret & Saucy Surprise. Angus sells the treasures of an Abbey to fund the monks' retirement, and a motoring fanatic's cheeky sculpture sets pulses racing on sale day. S6, Ep20
Debts To Settle. The Blacklighters are pushing for a big pay day. With weather threatening to halt their operation, the Misfits have to get crafty with their setup. S8, Ep18
